items.title: VI Live: Twente en Groningen trappen af in regenachtig Enschede
items.summary: Zondag staan er liefst vijf Eredivisie-duels op het programma. De wedstrijd van Feyenoord volg je in een apart liveblog, voor de rest kun je in deze VI Live terecht, net als voor al het buitenlandse voetbal.

items.title: Van Seumeren betreurt situatie Haller bij FC Utrecht: 'Gok verkeerd uitgepakt'
items.summary: De terugkeer van Sébastian Haller bij FC Utrecht is niet wat iedereen er tot dusver van hoopte. Eigenaar Frans van Seumeren vraagt zich bij Goedemorgen Eredivisie af of de 31-jarige spits überhaupt nog in actie komt dit seizoen.

items.title: Premier League news, Spurs v Arsenal buildup, Sheffield derby, and more – matchday live
items.summary: ⚽ Buildup to the weekend’s football action ⚽ Follow us over on Bluesky | And mail us here Liam Rosenior has revealed that one of his Chelsea stars marked the wrong Burnley player in added time yesterday, resulting in Zian Flemming’s equaliser. The Chelsea head coach said: “An assignment was missed. An assignment, a marking assignment wasn’t done. Flemming, we know, is their best header of the ball. Continue reading...

items.title: Van Seumeren over Utrecht-crisis: 'Geen twijfels aan Jans, wel peper in z'n kont'
items.summary: Met knappe uitzeges bij NEC en FC Groningen heeft FC Utrecht een periode van diepe crisis voorlopig het hoofd geboden. Clubeigenaar Frans van Seumeren vertelde bij ESPN dat er geen twijfel is geweest aan het functioneren van Ron Jans.

items.title: Van de Ven betreurt vertrek Frank bij Spurs en looft Tudor
items.summary: Micky van de Ven heeft voor het eerst gereageerd op het ontslag van manager Thomas Frank bij Tottenham Hotspur. De Oranje-international bespreekt ook de nieuwe interim-manager bij de huidige nummer zestien van de Premier League.

items.title: De last dance van Ricardo Pepi in het shirt van PSV
items.summary: Na een onderarmbreuk is Ricardo Pepi (23) weer inzetbaar voor PSV en zijn rentree vierde hij met een doelpunt tegen Heerenveen. Intussen hoopt Fulham, na een mislukte flirt in de winter, alsnog met de Amerikaanse spits aan de haal te gaan.

items.title: Man City's 'massive' win piles pressure on Arsenal
items.summary: Pep Guardiola hailed a "massive" win after title-chasing Manchester City held on for a crucial yet nervy victory over Newcastle on Saturday.

items.title: Arteta: Players must accept 'noise' in title race
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has told his title-chasing players to join another club if they can't handle the "noise and bullets" that comes with fighting for the Premier League.

items.title: Klopp: Liverpool success impossible without Milner
items.summary: Jürgen Klopp has said that James Milner's achievement of breaking the Premier League appearance record is unlikely to ever be broken, and praised him for his Liverpool input.

items.title: LAFC down Miami in season opener at Coliseum
items.summary: Inter Miami's defense of its MLS Cup title began in disappointing fashion, with a 3-0 defeat to LAFC at the LA Coliseum on Saturday.

items.title: World No 1 Thitikul wins home LPGA Thailand tournament
items.summary: World No 1 Jeeno Thitikul justified her top billing in emphatic fashion after edging Japan's Chizzy Iwai by one stroke to claim her home LPGA Thailand tournament for the first time.

items.title: VAR review: Did City push a player offside? Bernardo lucky to avoid red?
items.summary: Newcastle's Dan Burn had a goal ruled out for offside after being pushed by Man City defender Rúben Dias, while Bernado Silva avoided a second yellow card for jumping into him.

items.title: Scorecard: England vs Sri Lanka, T20 World Cup
England post below par target as Sri Lanka set to bat in T20 World Cup LIVE!
‘You need to be a team’: Rowett urges unity as depleted Leicester show fight
items.summary: Gary Rowett has told his Leicester players to deprioritise ‘individual ambitions’ after Saturday’s 2-2 draw with Stoke By the stage Ben Nelson headed against the bar in stoppage time and then Luke Thomas nodded against the inside of a post, it felt as if Leicester City were missing the chance to move into the promotion playoffs, so dominant had they been in the second half of this absorbing 2-2 draw with Stoke City. Yet after this month’s six-point deduction for breaching the Premier League’s profitability and sustainability rules, against which they are appealing, Ben Wilmot’s 89th-minute equaliser dropped Leicester back into the Championship’s relegation zone, placing the challenge facing them and their new manager, Gary Rowett, into stark context. Continue reading...

items.title: Today on Sky Sports Racing: Potters Charm seeks National Spirit gold at Fontwell
items.summary: Potters Charm headlines Sunday afternoon's feature National Spirit Hurdle , live on Sky Sports Racing.

items.title: Rugby League in Las Vegas 2026: Everything you need to know
items.summary: Rugby league once again hits US soil on Saturday February 28 into Sunday March 1 for a thrilling festival of action and Sky Sports is bringing you every game from Sin City.

items.title: Football quiz: Name the north London derby's top scorers
items.summary: With the north London derby coming up on Sunday, can you name the top scorers across Arsenal and Tottenham's Premier League meetings?

items.title: Guardiola tells team to chill with cocktails as Man City pile pressure on Arsenal
items.summary: As Manchester City continue to crank up the pressure on Arsenal, Pep Guardiola tells his players to take three days off to "enjoy life" with "caipirinhas and daiquiris".

items.title: ‘Caipirinhas, daiquiris’: Guardiola tells City players to chill and ignore title pressure
items.summary: Manchester City beat Newcastle to cut Arsenal’s lead to two Team given three days off before facing Leeds Pep Guardiola told his players to “take a lot of caipirinhas, daiquiris” to handle the pressure of the title chase after Manchester City beat Newcastle 2-1 to close to within two points of Arsenal. Two goals from Nico O’Reilly on 14 and 27 minutes at the Etihad Stadium were answered only by Lewis Hall’s strike in between as City moved to 56 points and a goal difference of 31, one fewer than Arsenal’s. Continue reading...
